params.description: Data table appears showing Marbles in Jars data: Jar A=7, Jar B=12, Jar C=19, Jar D=23.
Horizontal bar graph appears. Marbles in Jars data. Scale of 1, axis 0–23 with 24 tick marks visible.

beats.text: Scale of 1 works, but that's a lot of lines. You learned bigger scales are usually easier to read.

elements.description: Data table showing Marbles in Jars values: Jar A=7, Jar B=12, Jar C=19, Jar D=23.
Horizontal bar graph. Marbles in Jars. Scale of 1, axis 0–23, 24 tick marks. Four bars displayed.

params.description: Graph updates to scale of 10. Bars appear with ⚠️ indicators on Jar A and Jar C. Jar A (7) between 0 and 10. Jar C (19) between 10 and 20. Axis shows 0, 10, 20, 30—3 tick marks.

elements.description: Data table showing Marbles in Jars values: Jar A=7, Jar B=12, Jar C=19, Jar D=23.
Horizontal bar graph. Marbles in Jars. Scale of 10, axis 0–30, 4 tick marks. ⚠️ indicators on Jar A and Jar C bars.

beats.text: Hmm. Scale of 10 fits, but there are only 3 marks on the axis. Look at those warnings. Can you tell the exact value of where those bars end? Someone looking at our graph without the data table would need to guess how many marbles are shown.

params.description: ⚠️ on Jar A highlights with message: '7 won't land exactly on a line or in the middle.'

elements.description: Data table showing Marbles in Jars values: Jar A=7, Jar B=12, Jar C=19, Jar D=23.
Horizontal bar graph. Marbles in Jars. Scale of 10, axis 0–30. ⚠️ on Jar A showing exactness warning message.

beats.text: For big data, we'd pick Scale of 10 anyway. It's the easiest to read because we don't want too many lines on the axis. But these numbers are small and it's hard to find the exact location of the bar on the graph. We can do better.
When data is small, you have room to find a scale where everything lands exactly.

elements.description: Data table showing Marbles in Jars values: Jar A=7, Jar B=12, Jar C=19, Jar D=23.
Horizontal bar graph. Marbles in Jars. Scale of 10, axis 0–30, 4 tick marks. ⚠️ indicators on Jar A and Jar C.
